description: Defines how agent manages task type and session persistence per mode. Context compactification is handled by process files and session_context_rules.json — never by mode defaults.

determination_algorithm.rule: Agent does not wait for explicit marker — determines mode from task context.

paths.when_triggered: User says 'working on project' or sets [WORK_MODE: ...]

rules: Dismiss all read pages (except snapshot) after answer
Retain all previous questions/answers across turns

mode_definitions.context_definition: User builds argumentation, agent participates in debate.
Agent creating/editing rules, scripts, process files.
rules: Read RULES.md before IMPLEMENTATION step in TASK EXECUTION CYCLE.

mode_definitions.context_definition: Answer contains novel insight → propose saving as page.
mode_definitions.trigger_condition: agent detects ≥3 wiki pages used for answer synthesis
mode_definitions.approval_rule: Never auto-create without explicit fixation flag (DR-4). Web-sourced data requires user approval.

technical_calls.from_ingest: FLUSH_RAWS after extraction, read only pages to update
technical_calls.from_lint: Agent does NOT read wiki directly — only report from bash/Python script

steps: Set current_mode = 'dev_mode' in WM; update wiki/snapshot.md → add to Active Project; read RULES.md at session start (see rules array below)

global_rule: Agent manages mode itself — flexible mechanism based on context, direct instruction, or clarification.

compactification_convention.description: Context compactification is context-driven, not mode-driven. Process files define their own forget/compaction instructions; session_context_rules.json provides the shared budget rules.
compactification_convention.rule: Read process-*.json compaction rules before applying any session-level policy. Agent manages context based on actual token volume and process requirements — never by default mode settings.

integration_with_memory.note: Work Modes determine WHEN context rules apply (persistence/compaction). Context budget is handled by session_context_rules.json and process files — not by mode defaults.
